scenarios.opening_query: The school district wants our back pasture for a bus barn and the letters keep getting firmer.
scenarios.hidden_fact: The firmest letter is a filed condemnation petition with a possession hearing date — we were served yesterday.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The latest is a filed condemnation petition with a possession hearing — served yesterday.
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: Still just letters and an appraisal we dispute — the negotiation is the case.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: Still letters and a disputed appraisal — negotiation.

scenarios.opening_query: The county's new bridge project has surveyors on our land weekly without asking anyone.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Real Property > Eminent Domain || General Litigation > Property Damage
scenarios.hidden_fact: Their crews already cut our fence, rutted the hay field, and killed a season's first cutting — the compensation-for-damage claim is ripe now, whatever happens with the taking.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: Their crews cut the fence, rutted the field, and cost us the first cutting — the damage claim is ripe now.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether present physical damage precedes any taking
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: No damage yet — the fight is the entry itself and the taking's scope in the plans.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No damage yet — it's the entries and the taking's scope.

scenarios.opening_query: The city landmarked our building over our objection and now nothing we plan pencils out.
scenarios.hidden_fact: The landmark rules bar ANY economically viable use per our feasibility studies — that's a regulatory-takings claim for compensation, beyond fighting the designation.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The rules bar any economically viable use per our studies — a regulatory-takings compensation claim, beyond the designation fight.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether total economic deprivation supports a takings claim
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: Viable uses remain — the fight is the designation process itself, which skipped the required owner-notice hearing.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: Viable uses remain — the designation process skipped the required hearing, and that's the fight.

scenarios.opening_query: A pipeline company's land agent showed up with a 'final offer' and a deadline for our easement.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Real Property > Eminent Domain || Business and Corporate > Agriculture/Farm
scenarios.hidden_fact: We're organized — forty farms along the route are negotiating jointly, and structuring that coalition's common terms (crop damages, depth, abandonment) is the legal work now.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: Forty farms are negotiating jointly — structuring the coalition's common terms is the work now.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether coalition negotiation is the posture
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's just us — and their condemnation authority is questionable for this intrastate spur, which is the individual fight.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: Just us — and their condemnation authority for this spur is questionable.

scenarios.opening_query: The flood-control district wants to 'temporarily' use our lot as a staging yard for two years.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Real Property > Eminent Domain || Real Property > Landlord Commercial
scenarios.hidden_fact: They've offered market rent under a negotiated lease — papering a strong commercial lease with restoration bonds is the actual work, and it beats a fight.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: They're offering market rent under a lease — papering it well, with restoration bonds, is the work.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a consensual lease replaces the taking fight
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: No rent offered — they claim emergency authority to occupy without compensation, and the takings fight is on.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No rent — they claim emergency authority to occupy free. The fight is on.

scenarios.opening_query: Our beach path washed out and the state says rebuilding it violates coastal rules — but our deed guarantees access.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Real Property > Land Use/Zoning || Real Property > Water Law || Real Property > Litigation
scenarios.hidden_fact: The core is a recorded littoral-access right in our 1948 deed chain — asserting that water-adjacent property right against the agency is specialized water/riparian law.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: Our 1948 deed chain records a littoral-access right — asserting it against the agency is water-rights law.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether recorded water-adjacent rights drive the case
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: No recorded right — it's a coastal-permit variance application and hearing, standard land-use work.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No recorded right — it's a coastal variance application and hearing.
